signals.headline: Key details:** Monthly CPI rose 0
signals.raw: Key details:** Monthly CPI rose 0.3% (below 0.6% expected), core inflation (ex-food/energy) fell to 2.2% YoY (vs 2.3% prior, 2.1% expected), with transportation up 5% but food down to 0.5%[1]. Bank of Korea expected to hold rates at 2.5% in April, adopting wait-and-see amid contained pressures despite Middle East oil shocks[1][2][5]. Inflation expectations ticked up to 2.7%[3].
